item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is emotionally taxing to read, but in a good way. The distress of SCP-3578 is palpable, and it invokes empathy. The Foundation in this article may be even too accomodating for such a resource-heavy object, but this could be attributed to a long-running project that the higher-ups wanted to bring to fruitition due to curiosity.

The real problem here is the length. The numerous descriptions of different SCP-3578-A instances, despite being abridged, is a heavy read. There are some other issues that I can dismiss as stylistic preferences or an invocation of 'there is no canon', and as such I won't address.

All in all, while the feeling is all there, I will novote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great SCP. +1. There is a problem though. It says SCP-3578-A-31 was born September **, 199*, and the interview was three years later on September **, 200*. Problem is the interview date says 199* for the year. That should be fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, I love this one. It may be a bit long, but that's true for my own SCP draft so.

Anyways, I loved both the concept and the slight creepiness that came from it. And oh dear, the children…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting idea. Liked it on the sandbox, so nice to see it here. Hope it doesn't get downvoted to death.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's a bit too long, and there is a few places where things can be removed for redundancy.

+ show block
– hide block

SCP-3578 is to be kept in a 7m x 7m General-Purpose Containment Chamber that is conjoined to an adjacent 10m x 10m Humanoid Containment Chamber

and later

8m x 8m Standard Humanoid Containment Chamber

Are those specific dimensions necessary? Probably not - I'd replace 7x7 m and 8x8 m with "Standard" and 10x10 with "Standard Large" or something similar.

Both rooms are to be well maintained and cleaned regularly to prevent bacteria growth that may be caused by the buildup of mucus and discharge from SCP-3578.

Unless you specify the interval in which cleaning must occur, (e.g. daily) this might not be necessary as it's standard procedure to keep things clean when possible. It's called Special Containment Procedures, after all. I might be wrong here, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you very much for the feedback!

Yeah, I was pretty confident with this one so I hope it gets a bit more positive reception. I wish people who didn't enjoy it would give some feedback so I know what to improve, as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dont see why this is getting downvoted to be perfectly honest.

Its long, sure, but it's very interesting. I liked the ending, how the meaning was conveyed through -31 to the research team.

Mightve been a few errors in spelling and grammar, but I didn't notice anything, and wasnt broken on the immersion from anything.

I hope it sticks around, or at least people would explain why they dont like it.
